router.details: GET /parcels/{official_parcel_id} uses response_model=ParcelDetailResponse and returns ParcelService.get_parcel_detail().

repository.details: ParcelRepository.get_by_official_parcel_id computes ST_PointOnSurface-derived longitude/latitude and Box2D extent values from public.parcels_enriched.geometry.

service.details: ParcelService.get_parcel_detail maps centroid and extent values into ParcelMapFocus and includes map_focus in ParcelDetailResponse.

schema.details: ParcelDetailResponse exposes map_focus: ParcelMapFocus with centroid, extent, spatial_reference, geometry_available, and full_geometry_returned.

duplicate_backend_scan.conclusion: No duplicate executable CFS backend source tree was found inside the repository.

root_cause.description: The uvicorn process serving port 8000 was running from the correct backend directory, but it was started before the source code was updated with map_focus. Because it was not running with reload, it continued serving the old imported schema and route response.

recommendations: For active local development, start FastAPI with --reload from C:\CabarrusFutureScape\backend so backend code changes are picked up automatically.
Before testing frontend map focus, ensure the frontend is started with NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_BACKEND_API=true and NEXT_PUBLIC_CFS_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000.
If the endpoint appears stale again, compare /openapi.json with backend/app/schemas/parcel.py first; OpenAPI quickly reveals whether the running process imported the current schema.
